heading: Climate Security Advisory Council
label: 3060 Climate Security Advisory Council

E, title LIII, § 5321(c)133 Stat. 2129Dec. 20, 2019“Not later than 90 days after the date of the enactment of this Act [], the Director of National Intelligence shall appoint the members of the Council under section 120 of the National Security Act of 1947 [], as added by subsection (a).”Dec. 20, 201950 U.S.C. 3060

children.text: assisting intelligence analysts of various elements of the intelligence community with respect to analysis of climate security and its impact on the areas of focus of such analysts;

facilitating coordination between the elements of the intelligence community and elements of the Federal Government that are not elements of the intelligence community in collecting data on, and conducting analysis of, climate change and climate security; and
ensuring that the intelligence community is adequately prioritizing climate change in carrying out its activities.

children.label: A
children.text: An appropriate official from the National Intelligence Council, who shall chair the Council.

children.label: i
children.text: the Central Intelligence Agency;

children.label: ii
children.text: the Bureau of Intelligence and Research of the Department of State;

children.label: iii
children.text: the National Geospatial-Intelligence Agency;

children.label: iv
children.text: the Office of Intelligence and Counterintelligence of the Department of Energy;

children.label: v
children.text: the Office of the Under Secretary of Defense for Intelligence and Security; and

children.label: vi
children.text: the Defense Intelligence Agency.

children.label: i
children.text: providing decision makers with a predictive understanding of the climate;

children.label: ii
children.text: making observations of our Earth system that can be used by the public, policymakers, and to support strategic decisions; or

children.label: iii
children.text: coordinating Federal research and investments in understanding the forces shaping the global environment, both human and natural, and their impacts on society.

children.label: D
children.text: Any other officials as the Director of National Intelligence or the chair of the Council may determine appropriate.

children.label: A
children.text: identifying agencies to supply individuals from elements of the Federal Government that are not elements of the intelligence community;

children.label: B
children.text: securing the permission of the relevant agency heads for the participation of such individuals on the Council; and

children.label: C
children.text: any other duties that the Director of National Intelligence may direct.

children.heading: Duties and responsibilities of Council
children.label: c Duties and responsibilities of Council
A
children.text: exchange appropriate data between elements of the intelligence community and elements of the Federal Government that are not elements of the intelligence community;

children.label: B
children.text: discuss processes for the routine exchange of such data and implementation of such processes; and

children.label: C
children.text: prepare summaries of the business conducted at each meeting.

To assess and determine best practices with respect to the analysis of climate security, including identifying publicly available information and intelligence acquired through clandestine means that enables such analysis.
To assess and identify best practices with respect to prior efforts of the intelligence community to analyze climate security.
To assess and describe best practices for identifying and disseminating climate intelligence indications and warnings.
To recommend methods of incorporating analysis of climate security and the best practices identified under paragraphs (2) through (4) into existing analytic training programs.

To consult, as appropriate, with other elements of the intelligence community that conduct analysis of climate change or climate security and elements of the Federal Government that are not elements of the intelligence community that conduct analysis of climate change or climate security, for the purpose of sharing information about ongoing efforts and avoiding duplication of existing efforts.

children.label: A
children.text: to exchange appropriate data between such elements, establish processes, procedures and practices for the routine exchange of such data, discuss the implementation of such processes; and

children.label: B
children.text: to enable and facilitate the sharing of findings and analysis between such elements.

To assess whether the elements of the intelligence community that conduct analysis of climate change or climate security may inform the research direction of academic work and the sponsored work of the United States Government.

At the discretion of the chair of the Council, to convene conferences of analysts and nonintelligence community personnel working on climate change or climate security on subjects that the chair shall direct.

children.heading: Requirement
children.label: 1 Requirement
children.text: Not later than , and not less frequently than annually thereafter, the chair of the Council shall submit, on behalf of the Council, to the congressional intelligence committees a report describing the activities of the Council as described in subsection (c) during the year preceding the year during which the report is submitted.January 31, 2021

children.label: A
children.text: the Council fulfilling its duties and responsibilities under subsection (c); or

children.label: B
children.text: the responsiveness of the intelligence community to the climate security needs and priorities of the policymaking elements of the Federal Government.

children.heading: Sunset
children.label: e Sunset
children.text: The Council shall terminate on .December 31, 2024

children.label: A
children.text: The national security of the United States, including national security infrastructure.

children.label: B
children.text: Subnational, national, and regional political stability.

children.label: C
children.text: The security of allies and partners of the United States.

children.label: D
children.text: Ongoing or potential political violence, including unrest, rioting, guerrilla warfare, insurgency, terrorism, rebellion, revolution, civil war, and interstate war.

children.label: A
children.text: imminently and substantially alter the political stability or degree of human security in a country or region; or

children.label: i
children.text: the national security of the United States;

children.label: ii
children.text: the military, political, or economic interests of allies and partners of the United States; or

children.label: iii
children.text: citizens of the United States abroad.
